business_hours.seasonal_hours: Tom mentioned possibly adding Saturday hours next year but NOT yet. Do not configure Saturday hours.

contact_info.name: Linda
contact_info.context: Monitors the dispatch line until 8 PM for after-hours emergencies and manages office operations.

contact_info.name: Jake
contact_info.context: Primary contact for after-hours plumbing emergencies.

contact_info.name: Steve
contact_info.context: Primary contact for after-hours HVAC emergencies.

contact_info.name: Tom
contact_info.context: Final backup contact for all after-hours emergencies.

contact_info.name: Linda Chen
contact_info.context: Primary contact for incoming calls and dispatch coordination during business hours and until 8 PM on business days.

contact_info.name: Tom Chen
contact_info.context: Handles all gas leak calls personally, final escalation for all other emergencies, and direct calls from clients asking for him.

contact_info.name: Jake
contact_info.context: First point of contact for plumbing emergencies after hours.

contact_info.name: Steve
contact_info.context: First point of contact for HVAC emergencies after hours.

services_supported: Tenant improvement build-outs (plumbing and HVAC portion)
HVAC installation, maintenance, and repair (commercial and some residential)
Water heater installation and repair (tank and tankless)
Hydronic heating system installation and service
Tenant improvement buildouts (plumbing, HVAC, and hydronic portions)
Colorado Master Plumber and HVAC contractor (licensed and insured)
services_excluded: Fire suppression or fire sprinkler work
emergency_definition: Burst or broken water pipes causing active flooding
Major water leaks from plumbing systems
Gas leaks or suspected gas leaks
No-heat situations when outdoor temperature is below 32 degrees Fahrenheit
Complete loss of hot water at large facilities (apartment complexes or hotels)
HVAC failures in critical environments (server rooms, medical facilities, laboratories)
Frozen pipes that haven't burst yet but need immediate attention to prevent bursting

order: For plumbing emergencies (burst pipes, water leaks, sewer, frozen pipes), attempt to transfer to Jake (on-call plumber) at 303-555-0134 for 30 seconds.
For HVAC emergencies (no heat, AC failure in critical areas, boiler issues), attempt to transfer to Steve (HVAC lead) at 303-555-0156 for 30 seconds.

For gas leaks, Clara must immediately instruct the caller to evacuate the building, not flip any light switches, not use the phone inside, get outside, and call 911 from outside FIRST. After this safety message, Clara should attempt to transfer to Tom Chen (Owner) at 303-555-0112.
If Jake or Steve do not answer within 30 seconds, escalate to Tom Chen (Owner) at 303-555-0112 as backup for all emergencies.
If Tom Chen does not answer after all attempts, take a detailed message.
emergency_routing_rules.fallback_action: If no one answers after all attempts, take a detailed message including the exact issue, property address, and property manager contact (if known). Send text alerts to Tom Chen, Linda Chen, and the relevant on-call technician (Jake or Steve depending on the trade).

non_emergency_routing_rules.action: Clara is to collect specific information from the caller.
info_to_collect: urgency (urgent for next business day or can wait)
non_emergency_routing_rules.callback_timeframe: We will call back in the morning (next business day).
non_emergency_routing_rules.notes: Clara should never promise a specific arrival time. Instead, use phrases like 'we'll dispatch someone as soon as possible'.

call_transfer_rules.transfer_fail_action: Take a message for Linda Chen, and she will call back within 30 minutes.
notes: For clients specifically asking for Tom Chen, Clara should attempt to transfer to his cell at 303-555-0112.
For anything billing-related, Clara should take a message and note it is for Amy in accounting.

integration_constraints: Evergreen Mechanical Services uses FieldEdge for dispatching and job tracking.
No Clara integration with FieldEdge is needed or desired.
Clara should not attempt to book jobs or access Evergreen Mechanical's schedule.
Clara's role is to collect information; scheduling is handled manually by Evergreen Mechanical staff.
after_hours_flow_summary: During after-hours (outside Mon-Fri, 7 AM-5:30 PM MT, and after 8 PM on business days, or all day weekends), Clara will first determine if the call is an emergency. If it's a gas leak, Clara will immediately deliver a critical safety message (evacuate, call 911) before attempting to transfer to Tom Chen. For other emergencies, Clara will follow the conditional emergency routing rules, attempting to reach Jake for plumbing issues or Steve for HVAC issues, with Tom Chen as the final escalation. If no one answers, Clara will take a detailed message and send text alerts to Tom, Linda, and the relevant on-call tech. For non-emergency calls after hours, Clara will collect the caller's name, phone number, company name (if commercial), property address, type of issue, and urgency, informing them that Evergreen Mechanical will call back the next business morning. On weekends, Linda Chen is skipped entirely for all calls, going straight to on-call techs and Tom for emergencies.
office_hours_flow_summary: During office hours (Monday through Friday, 7 AM to 5:30 PM Mountain Time), Clara will primarily attempt to transfer all incoming calls to Linda Chen (Office Manager) at 303-555-0189. If Linda does not answer within 20 seconds, Clara will take a message, and Linda will commit to calling back within 30 minutes. For clients specifically requesting Tom Chen, Clara will attempt to transfer to his cell at 303-555-0112. For billing-related inquiries, Clara will take a message for Amy in accounting. If an emergency call comes in during office hours, Clara will follow the same emergency routing rules as after-hours, attempting to reach the appropriate on-call technician (Jake or Steve) or Tom Chen for gas leaks, with Tom as the general backup.
special_rules: Clara must never promise a specific arrival time for technicians. Instead, use phrases like 'we'll dispatch someone as soon as possible'.

For calls reporting a gas smell, Clara must immediately interrupt the call to deliver a critical safety message: 'Leave the building, don't flip any light switches, don't use the phone inside, get outside, call 911 from outside, and THEN we can help.' This is a priority override.
Do not configure Saturday hours at this time, as they are not yet implemented by Evergreen Mechanical Services.
notes: Updated with onboarding data. Operational details confirmed.
